text: Arabic as written, spoken or adjectives language has its own characteristics. Like that, there are many fields of knowledge of Arabic. For every field of knowledge of Arabic, many subjects will be studied. In the field of Arabic morphology that is also called shorof science in universities, this study aimed at identifying linguistic features found in surah An-Naba' in the Holy Qur'an. The library research method is used in this study. The objective of this study is to determine the number of words of fi’il mudhori that exist in Surah An-naba (juz 30) and how these words are read as well as to identify the dhomir that exist in every sentence of the word of fi’il mudhori and described it by using methods of nahwu and shorof science. And the conclusion reached from the result of this study shows that the researcher found out that there are 16 words in surah An-Naba' that consists of fi'il mudhari. These 16 words are scattered on various patterns of wazans and dhomir-dhomir.
explanation: The text contains accurate domain terms and a coherent scholarly structure, which hints at human authorship. However, the occasional awkward phrasing and transliteration quirks could also appear in AI-generated text, yielding a moderate probability.
